title: Maths and Stats for AI Engineers

slides.title: Vectors and Cosine Similarity
slides.content:
Vectors represent data in n-dimensional space. The distance or angle between vectors defines their similarity.

<svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="5" refY="5" markerWidth="4" markerHeight="4" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="currentColor" />
    </marker>
  </defs>
  <line x1="20" y1="180" x2="180" y2="180" stroke="gray" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)" />
  <line x1="20" y1="180" x2="20" y2="20" stroke="gray" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)" />
  <line x1="20" y1="180" x2="120" y2="60" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="3" marker-end="url(#arrow)" />
  <text x="125" y="55" fill="#3b82f6" font-size="14" font-family="sans-serif">Vector A</text>
  <line x1="20" y1="180" x2="160" y2="140" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="3" marker-end="url(#arrow)" />
  <text x="165" y="145" fill="#ef4444" font-size="14" font-family="sans-serif">Vector B</text>
  <path d="M 60 132 A 60 60 0 0 0 74 115" fill="none" stroke="#10b981" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="75" y="135" fill="#10b981" font-size="14" font-family="sans-serif">θ</text>
</svg>

slides.title: Matrix Transformations
slides.content:
A matrix can transform a vector by scaling, rotating, or translating it.

<svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <g transform="translate(100,100)">
    <line x1="-80" y1="0" x2="80" y2="0" stroke="#e5e7eb" stroke-width="1"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="-80" x2="0" y2="80" stroke="#e5e7eb" stroke-width="1"/>
    <rect x="0" y="-50" width="50" height="50" fill="#3b82f6" fill-opacity="0.3" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/>
    <polygon points="0,0 70,-20 50,-90 -20,-70" fill="#ef4444" fill-opacity="0.3" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/>
    <text x="5" y="-55" fill="#3b82f6" font-size="12" font-family="sans-serif">Original</text>
    <text x="20" y="-95" fill="#ef4444" font-size="12" font-family="sans-serif">Transformed</text>
  </g>
</svg>

slides.title: Normal Distribution and Percentiles
slides.content:
Understanding Mean and P99 to gauge application performance accurately.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <line x1="20" y1="180" x2="280" y2="180" stroke="#374151" stroke-width="2"/>
  <path d="M 30 180 Q 80 180 100 100 T 150 20 T 200 100 T 270 180" fill="none" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="3"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="180" x2="150" y2="20" stroke="#9ca3af" stroke-dasharray="5,5"/>
  <text x="135" y="195" font-size="12" font-family="sans-serif" fill="#4b5563">Mean</text>
  <line x1="220" y1="180" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-dasharray="5,5"/>
  <text x="210" y="195" font-size="12" fill="#ef4444" font-family="sans-serif">P99</text>
</svg>

slides.title: Dimensionality Reduction (PCA)
slides.content:
Reducing data dimensions while preserving variance.

<svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <circle cx="50" cy="150" r="4" fill="#111827"/>
  <circle cx="70" cy="130" r="4" fill="#111827"/>
  <circle cx="90" cy="110" r="4" fill="#111827"/>
  <circle cx="110" cy="90" r="4" fill="#111827"/>
  <circle cx="130" cy="70" r="4" fill="#111827"/>
  <circle cx="150" cy="50" r="4" fill="#111827"/>
  <circle cx="60" cy="145" r="3" fill="#9ca3af"/>
  <circle cx="85" cy="120" r="3" fill="#9ca3af"/>
  <circle cx="100" cy="105" r="3" fill="#9ca3af"/>
  <circle cx="120" cy="85" r="3" fill="#9ca3af"/>
  <circle cx="140" cy="60" r="3" fill="#9ca3af"/>
  <line x1="30" y1="170" x2="170" y2="30" stroke="#8b5cf6" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="120" y="25" fill="#8b5cf6" font-size="12" font-family="sans-serif">Principal Component</text>
</svg>
